title: They Said I Was Building Another Buffer Clone. They Were Wrong.
excerpt: For months everyone thought I was building just another social media scheduling tool. Here's what I was actually building.
content:
> **Quick Answer:** For months everyone thought I was building a Buffer clone. Here's what I was actually building: complete Twitter automation with Tweet Genie.

For months I've been hearing the same thing.

"Cool [scheduling](/blogs/guides/bulk-generation-14-day-playbook) tool."
"Isn't this just Buffer with AI?"
"Hootsuite already does this."
"The big players will just copy you the moment you get traction."

I smiled. Said nothing. And kept building.

Because what everyone saw was a [scheduling](/blogs/insights/what-reliable-social-automation-actually-needs) interface. What I was actually building was something else entirely.

---

## What You Saw vs What I Was Building

If you connected [TweetGenie](/blogs/guides/what-if-your-twitter-ran-itself-while-you-built-your-product) over the last few months, here's what you got:

A composer. A scheduler. [Bulk tweet generation](/blogs/guides/bulk-generation-14-day-playbook). [Analytics](/blogs/updates/team-mode-cross-posting-and-visibility-update-mar-2026). [Cross-posting to LinkedIn](/blogs/updates/linkedin-cross-posting-from-tweet-genie) and Threads. A [team dashboard with role-based access](/blogs/updates/team-mode-cross-posting-and-visibility-update-mar-2026). [Multi-account](/blogs/use-cases/agency-team-content-ops-use-case) management.

Useful tools. Good features. Nothing that would make Buffer lose sleep.

But that was never the product.

That was the test.

Every feature we shipped was stress-testing the architecture underneath. Multi-account management wasn't just a feature - it was proving we could isolate data cleanly across dozens of accounts at scale. Teams and RBAC wasn't just for agencies - it was proving the permission layer could handle complex access patterns without breaking. [Cross-posting](/blogs/updates/linkedin-cross-posting-from-tweet-genie) wasn't just convenience - it was proving the same action could fan out to multiple platforms simultaneously without failure.

50+ accounts. Months of real usage. Thousands of posts scheduled and published.

The foundation didn't just get built. It got proven.

And now we're building what it was always designed for.

---

## What's Actually Coming - Complete Social Media Automation

Not "smarter [scheduling](/blogs/updates/cross-posting-scheduling-reliability-roundup-feb-2026)."

Not "AI that writes tweets for you."

Complete. Automation.

Here's what that actually means for you.

**You connect once.**

[TweetGenie](/blogs/updates/twitter-complete-automation-coming-soon) analyses your real account. Your actual tweets. Your actual engagement data. What performed. What flopped. What's trending in your exact niche right now across the entire web.

It doesn't ask you generic questions like "what's your niche?" It tells YOU what your niche is - based on evidence, not guesswork.

Your [content strategy gets built automatically](/blogs/guides/how-to-automate-twitter-posts) in 3 minutes. Not from a template someone made for a generic SaaS founder. From your account. Your data. Your audience.

**Then every Monday morning - without you opening a single app - it generates your entire week of content.**

Seven tweets. Tailored to your [strategy](/blogs/story/from-content-chaos-to-system-why-we-built-suitegenie). Informed by what performed last week. Tuned to what's trending in your space right now.

You review in 2 minutes. Approve. Done.

Or you don't review at all.

**Turn on autopilot.** Content gets generated, scheduled, and posted without you touching anything. You get a notification. You have one hour to undo anything. After that it just runs.

Your Twitter grows while you do literally anything else.

**And here's the part that nobody - not Buffer, not Hootsuite, not any of them - has built:**

It learns.

Every week [TweetGenie](/blogs/updates/twitter-complete-automation-feature-drop-what-we-are-building) analyses what performed and what didn't. Which topics landed. Which formats your audience responded to. Which posting times drove the most reach.

Next week's content is generated based on that data.

- Week 1 - good content.
- Week 4 - great content.
- Week 8 - content that consistently outperforms everyone else in your niche.

The longer you use it the smarter it gets. Every single week.

---

## Why Buffer Can't Just Copy This

I want to address this directly because I've heard it from investors, from fellow founders, from people who genuinely care.

"What stops Buffer from just building this?"

A few things.

**First - architecture.**

Buffer was built as a scheduling tool. Their entire codebase, their data model, their infrastructure - it's optimised for "take this content and post it at this time." Bolting a learning AI layer on top of that architecture is like trying to add a turbo engine to a bicycle. You can do it. But you'll rebuild most of the bicycle in the process.

We built the engine first. Then we built the bicycle around it.

**Second - the feedback loop takes time.**

The self-learning engine isn't magic. It needs real performance data from real accounts over real weeks to generate meaningful insights. We have 50+ accounts already running on our infrastructure. We have months of engagement data already flowing into the system.

Buffer starting today would be 6 months behind on data alone.

**Third - they're not incentivised to.**

Buffer makes money on seats and accounts. They have enterprise contracts and quarterly targets. Shipping a feature that fundamentally changes their [pricing](/blogs/comparisons/suitegenie-vs-hootsuite) model and cannibalises their existing product is politically hard inside a company that size.

We have nothing to protect. We can build the thing that actually solves the problem.

---

## This Is Just Twitter

Here's what I haven't told you yet.

The same infrastructure that powers [Twitter automation](/blogs/guides/bulk-content-planning-for-founders-and-small-teams) - the multi-account layer, the teams and permissions, the cross-posting engine, the analytics sync, the AI content generation - all of it runs [LinkedIn Genie](/blogs/updates/linkedin-genie-strategy-builder-and-tweet-genie-rollout) and the Meta integration too.

That wasn't an accident. That was the plan from day one.

Once [Twitter automation](/blogs/guides/how-to-automate-twitter-posts) is complete - and it's shipping in phases right now - LinkedIn automation follows. Then Meta.

One platform. Every major social network. Complete automation.

**For solo founders this means:**
Your Twitter, LinkedIn, and Instagram all growing simultaneously. Based on their own strategies. Learning from their own data. Running themselves.

**For agencies this means:**
One person managing 10 client accounts across three platforms. Content generated. Scheduled. Posted. Learned from. All without a content writer, a strategist, or a scheduler.

We're not building a better Buffer.

We're building what comes after Buffer.

---

## Why I'm Telling You This Now

Because the accounts that start now win.

Phase 1 ships very soon. The moment you connect your account, TweetGenie starts building your strategy from real data. By the time Phase 5 ships - the self-learning engine - the accounts that started early will have months of performance data feeding into it.

The learning engine gets smarter the longer it runs. The gap between early accounts and late accounts will be real and it will compound every single week.

The accounts that sign up today won't just get a better tool.

They'll get a head start that nobody who joins later can close.

---

## Sign Up Free at suitegenie.in

No waitlist. No credit card. No 14-day trial that expires when you finally remember to actually use it.

**[Create your account](https://suitegenie.in/register)**. Connect your Twitter. Get early access to Phase 1 the moment it drops.

Then watch what happens when your social media runs itself.

Follow every phase as it ships: **[@suitegenie](https://twitter.com/suitegenie)** on Twitter.

---

*Built by Kanishk Saraswat - founder, builder, someone who stayed up till 4am debugging so you don't have to think about Twitter ever again.*

---
